parameters.content:
## Universal Auto-Responder
### WhatsApp · Instagram · Facebook

**One trigger, three channels.** A user messages any connected channel → instant reply. No per-channel code.

- **Text** → echoed back.
- **Anything else** (image, audio, video, document…) → a reply describing the attachment.

### Before you start
**Connect at least one channel first** — use the *Connect a Channel* workflow, or the [Fiwano portal](https://fiwano.com). The responder wires itself to channels you've already connected; with none connected, there's nothing to reply on.

### Setup
1. Add your **Fiwano API** credential to the Trigger and both reply nodes.
2. *(Recommended)* Set a **Webhook Secret** on that **credential** — the Trigger uses it to verify incoming signatures. (The node's own Webhook Secret field is just an override; leave it empty to use the credential's.)
3. **Save & Activate.**

### What activation does
The Trigger is set to **Auto-Setup → All Active Channels**: on activation it points **every active channel** at this workflow at once — registering its webhook URL, the selected **Event Types**, and the credential's secret. Connect a channel later? Re-activate to wire it. Deactivating clears the webhook again.

Keep **Event Types** on *Message Received*. n8n must be publicly reachable.

[Docs](https://fiwano.com/documentation/n8n) · [Get an API key](https://fiwano.com)

### 📥 What's in each message

Same shape for all 3 channels (`$json` on the trigger):

- `channel_id` — which channel
- `data.from` — sender → use as reply recipient
- `data.from_name` — name (WhatsApp only; `null` on IG/FB)
- `data.type` — `text`, `image`, `audio`, `video`, `document`…
- `data.text` — the text (when `type = text`)
- `data.media` — `media_id`, `mime_type`, `file_size`, `filename`… (for media)

## 📎 Want to echo the file back?

The **attachment** reply just describes the file. To send the **actual file** back (a full media echo) you need a **Pro** license + object storage:

1. **Fiwano → Media → Download** — pulls the file into n8n. (The inbound `download_url` is API-key-protected, so Meta can't fetch it directly.)
2. **Re-host** it at a public/signed HTTPS URL — S3, GCS, Cloudflare R2, MinIO, Azure SAS… Meta fetches outbound media **by URL**, so this step is required.
3. **Fiwano → Message → Send Media** with that URL.

Add those three nodes after the *attachment* branch when you need it.
